threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the pavement when a dog’s on a lead, while locals casually sidestep like it’s a game of Frogger.
Caught a glimpse of a cat being walked on a leash at 2am near Brixton Market. Only in South London, right? Cat was clearly running the show.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found an old lady feeding the pigeons in the park while arguing with a mural about her ex. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar with a pet pig as the mascot. It lounges freely, soaking in the attention while the beer flows.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local if they know the best spot to grab a burrito after 2 AM and if they side-eye tourists trying to navigate the bus map.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning and a guy in full clown attire got on, juggling while the bus lurched. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just hop on any tram and end up at Parc Güell. Meanwhile, locals know the best views are from the secret spots off Carrer de Progrés.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they struggle with the siesta schedule; it’s 4 PM and they're still looking for lunch.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus even when it's clearly marked as 'Not in Service.' Everyone else just walks on.
Caught a night bus past the old biscuit factory, saw a group of ravers arguing with a seagull over a discarded kebab. Only in Bermondsey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I watched a guy order a Döner in perfect Turkish, then debate the best vegan brunch spots for an hour with a friend. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people bringing their own utensils to the park for a picnic. Makes a casual stroll feel like a gourmet event.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a real local knows to avoid BART on Mondays after equipment problems. Tourists just stand confused by the delays while we know the back roads.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a busker playing accordion while a couple danced by the back door. Classic San Francisco, where every ride's a mini street fair.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are in the tourist traps. The real gems are the hidden bars where you can hear locals gossip over a glass of vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by the way they still hesitate at the unmarked crosswalks, waiting for a green light to cross.
